global.title: Trump Heads to China Amid Iran Stalemate, Markets React to Fed - Global Banking & Finance Review
global.summary: Trump’s China visit is unfolding against an Iran war stalemate, rare-earth supply concerns, and renewed market anxiety over inflation and interest rates.
global.whyItMatters: This shapes the global mood pastors preach into: uncertainty, economic strain, and rising geopolitical tension.

global.title: Post Market Wrap: May 12, 2026 - CNBC
global.summary: CNBC’s market close update highlights Senate confirmation of Kevin Warsh as Fed governor alongside continued investor focus on AI, inflation, and financial direction.
global.whyItMatters: Economic shifts affect household stress, giving pastors insight into the financial pressures many families may be carrying.

global.title: Oct. 7 sexual violence report author calls for action to prevent future atrocities - CNN
global.summary: A lead author on reporting into sexual violence tied to the Oct. 7 attacks is urging concrete action to prevent such atrocities from recurring.
global.whyItMatters: It is a sobering reminder to pray, speak carefully about trauma, and uphold the dignity of victims in public witness.

canadian.title: ‘A magnet for talent’: Scientists from around the world recruited by Toronto hospital network for medical research - CTV News
canadian.summary: Toronto’s University Health Network is drawing dozens of international scientists, strengthening Canada’s role in medical research and innovation.
canadian.whyItMatters: This offers a constructive national story about healing, discovery, and the common good—helpful for pastoral encouragement.

canadian.title: Global - Defense News
canadian.summary: Defense News reports Canada is looking to Turkey as a fellow mid-power partner in weapons development while broader Ukraine-related instability continues.
canadian.whyItMatters: It signals how Canada is responding to a less stable world, which can inform prayers for peace and discernment about national priorities.

persecutedChurch.title: J'lem church leaders urge Knesset to address 'hate crimes' against Christians - Haaretz
persecutedChurch.summary: Jerusalem church leaders are pressing Israel’s parliament to respond to hate crimes against Christians, signaling concern that harassment is becoming more visible and urgent.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a direct call from local Christian leaders for protection, prayer, and international attention.

persecutedChurch.title: Ihab Hassan: ‘No accountability’ over attacks on Palestinian Christians - Vatican News
persecutedChurch.summary: Vatican News highlights concerns from Palestinian Christian voices who say attacks and intimidation continue without meaningful accountability.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: It helps keep the suffering of a small and vulnerable Christian community before the church in prayer and advocacy.

leadership.title: Employee Engagement Hit A 10-Year Low. The Problem Isn't People — It's Organizational Systems. - Forbes
leadership.summary: Forbes argues that falling engagement is less about lazy people and more about unhealthy systems, weak psychological safety, and leadership that treats symptoms instead of causes.
leadership.whyItMatters: A useful lens for church leadership: culture, trust, and systems shape staff and volunteer flourishing more than slogans do.

goodNews.title: What went right this week: progress in tumultuous times, plus more
goodNews.summary: Positive News rounds up encouraging developments including health breakthroughs, conservation gains in Ukraine, and a victory for river defenders in the Amazon.
goodNews.whyItMatters: A helpful reminder that even in a heavy news cycle, signs of grace, courage, and renewal are still present.
